text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, little Mouse dreamed of adventure. He climbed up vines and jumped across fallen leaves. One leaf caught his tail. "Oh no!" he cried. But then he flytedHigher, discovering owl holes. He rested there quietly.

Suddenly rooster crowed loud. Owl frowned. "Stay quiet!"

Mouse did. Owls listened politely.

After hours, Mouse heard something rustle. He peeked. Owls!

He fled faster than wind.

Through pine needles he went.

Finding moss.

That's where he stayed.

Winter came gently.

Snow softly fell.

Mouse forgot fears.

Birds sang lullabies.

He fell asleep soundly.

Next morning star came.

Bright as bird.



That's my tale.
